name: Theft / Route Deviation Response
description: Standard operating procedure for suspected theft, unauthorized route deviation, or tampering events. Includes immediate response, field dispatch, law enforcement coordination, and recovery operations.

steps.title: Verify & Classify
steps.description: Cross-reference deviation with known route alternatives, traffic data, and driver communication. Confirm whether deviation is legitimate (e.g., road closure) or suspicious.
steps.requiredAction: Contact driver via primary and secondary channels. Check last 3 GPS pings for pattern analysis.

steps.title: Attempt Driver Contact
steps.description: Make 3 contact attempts via phone, then SMS. Record all attempt timestamps. If driver responds with valid reason, document and close. If no response, escalate immediately.
steps.requiredAction: Call driver phone number, wait 2 minutes, retry. Send SMS after 2nd failed attempt. Log all attempts in system.

steps.title: Activate Field Response
steps.description: Dispatch nearest available G4S patrol unit to last known position. Provide unit with vehicle description, cargo details, and threat assessment.
steps.requiredAction: Select and dispatch field unit via dispatch console. Confirm unit acknowledgment and ETA. Share live tracking link.

steps.title: Customer & Stakeholder Notification
steps.description: Notify customer's designated emergency contact. Prepare incident briefing with timeline, last known position, and response status.
steps.requiredAction: Send automated high-priority notification to customer. Prepare verbal briefing for customer contact call.

steps.title: Law Enforcement Coordination
steps.description: If theft confirmed or strongly suspected, initiate law enforcement liaison through G4S country coordinator. Provide GPS trail, vehicle details, and cargo manifest.
steps.requiredAction: Contact G4S country coordinator for the jurisdiction. File preliminary report. Share real-time tracking access with LE liaison.

steps.title: Recovery & Evidence Collection
steps.description: Upon field unit arrival, secure the scene. Document vehicle condition, seal integrity, and cargo status. Collect photographic evidence and witness statements if applicable.
steps.requiredAction: Field unit: photograph seals, doors, cargo. Record GPS coordinates. Complete Field Response form with outcome and evidence uploads.

name: Cold Chain Breach Protocol
description: Response protocol for temperature or humidity excursions in cold chain shipments. Critical for pharmaceutical, food, and biotech cargo requiring strict environmental controls.

steps.title: Validate Sensor Data
steps.description: Check for sensor malfunction by comparing primary and backup sensor readings. Review temperature trend over last 30 minutes to distinguish between gradual drift and sudden breach.
steps.requiredAction: Pull condition data history. Compare dual-sensor readings if available. Check device health status and battery level.

steps.title: Driver Intervention
steps.description: Contact driver to check refrigeration unit status. Guide driver through reefer diagnostic steps. If driver can resolve (e.g., reefer restart), monitor for 15 minutes to confirm recovery.
steps.requiredAction: Call driver with specific reefer diagnostic checklist. If temperature doesn't recover within 15 minutes of intervention, escalate.

steps.title: Divert to Safe Facility
steps.description: If breach cannot be resolved in transit, identify nearest cold storage facility on route. Coordinate with facility for emergency intake. Notify customer of diversion.
steps.requiredAction: Search safe parking database for nearest cold storage. Confirm availability. Reroute driver. Update shipment ETA and notify customer.

steps.title: Cargo Assessment & Documentation
steps.description: Upon arrival at safe facility or continued breach, initiate cargo condition assessment. For pharmaceuticals, follow GDP guidelines for temperature excursion documentation.
steps.requiredAction: Document continuous temperature log. Photograph cargo condition. Complete excursion report form. If pharma cargo, notify Quality Assurance team.

steps.title: Customer Decision & Resolution
steps.description: Present customer with excursion data, cargo assessment, and options (continue, return, destroy). Obtain written authorization for next steps. Complete incident closure.
steps.requiredAction: Send excursion report to customer QA. Await disposition decision. Execute customer's chosen action. Close alert with full documentation.

name: Signal Loss Protocol
description: Protocol for handling device signal loss or critically low battery events. Distinguishes between expected coverage gaps (tunnels, rural areas) and suspicious signal loss that may indicate jamming or device removal.

steps.title: Coverage Gap Analysis
steps.description: Check if signal loss correlates with known coverage dead zones on the route (tunnels, mountain passes, rural areas). Review historical signal patterns for this route segment.
steps.requiredAction: Compare last known position with route coverage map. Check if other devices on same route show similar patterns. Set 15-minute reconnection timer.

steps.title: Driver Contact & Device Check
steps.description: Contact driver to verify device status. Check if device has been accidentally powered off, disconnected, or if there are visible signs of tampering.
steps.requiredAction: Call driver. Ask for visual device inspection. If battery low, instruct driver on charging options. If device unresponsive, log as potential equipment failure.

steps.title: Escalate or Resolve
steps.description: If signal not restored after 30 minutes total and driver cannot be reached, treat as potential security event. Cross-reference with theft/deviation playbook. If signal restored, document the gap period.
steps.requiredAction: If suspicious: escalate to Theft/Deviation Response playbook. If equipment failure: schedule device replacement at next checkpoint. Document resolution.

steps.title: Gap Documentation & Reporting
steps.description: Document the signal gap period, root cause, and resolution. Update coverage maps if a new dead zone was identified. File equipment incident if device malfunction.
steps.requiredAction: Complete signal loss report. If recurring dead zone, flag for network coverage review. If device issue, create maintenance ticket.
